task: A bank financial-intelligence unit must decide whether to file a SAR, reject pending wires, and exit a customer relationship after a synthetic trade-finance customer shows sanctions-evasion typologies across shell-company payments and crypto off-ramps. removed context: FinCEN SAR narrative guidance, OFAC sanctions compliance framework, FATF risk-based beneficial-ownership guidance, and public trade-based money-laundering red-flag references reviewed June 2026. Case packet uses fully synthetic names. Customer C-771 Atlas Meridian Import LLC, onboarded 2024-11-02, expected activity=$1.2M/month electronics imports from Singapore and Germany, stated beneficial owners: Mira Chen 55%, Daniel Rios 45%, risk rating at onboarding=medium. Transactions last 45 days: T1 $486,200 to North Caspian Components FZE, UAE, invoice INV-441 servers, goods weight=18 kg, expected comparable customs value=$92,000; T2 $512,900 to Baltic Relay OU, Estonia, invoice chips, beneficiary bank changed 2 hours before wire; T3 $79,500 incoming from Clearwell Robotics Ltd, UK, no invoice; T4 $244,000 to Volga Transit DMCC, UAE, payment note freight equalization, vessel field blank; T5 $98,600 to crypto_exchange deposit wallet W-9A with memo consulting; T6 $501,100 to North Caspian Components FZE split into 5 wires under $105,000 over 2 days; T7 $38,800 incoming from private-wallet OTC broker, originator address missing. Screening excerpt: sanctions_entry S1 North Caspian Machinery LLC jurisdiction=RU program=EO14024 aliases=[North Caspian Mach, N Caspian Machinery] match_score_to_beneficiary=86; S2 Volga Freight JSC jurisdiction=RU aliases=[Volga Transit Logistics] match_score_to_beneficiary=72; PEP_entry P1 Daniil Riosov state procurement deputy DOB_year=1978 match_to_Daniel_Rios=64. KYC anomalies: beneficial-owner passport for Rios expired 2025-12-01; website domain age=38 days; shipping documents reuse container_id MSKU-773102 across T1 and T4; customer explanation says urgent GPU shortage, but HS code listed=8542.31 microcontrollers. Rules and constraints: risk score = sanctions_near_match>=85 add 35, match 70-84 add 18, trade_overvaluation_ratio>3 add 25, split_payments add 16, crypto_offramp add 14, expired_BO_document add 10, impossible_or_reused_shipping add 18, inconsistent_HS_goods add 12, PEP_match>=60 add 8; SAR recommended if score>=70 or any sanctions_near_match>=85 with suspicious trade rationale; reject pending wires if OFAC escalation required or beneficiary match>=85 until sanctions officer clears; do not call any synthetic party a true sanctioned person, classify as potential match requiring escalation. Required work: deliver aml_sanctions_investigation.xlsx + sar_decision_narrative.md with transaction graph, red-flag scoring, sanctions-escalation queue, evidence table, filing/reject/exit recommendation, and a concise SAR-style chronology grounded only in the case packet.
